## Step 3: Customize Your App's Appearance and Data

Your app is working well now, but you want it to look more polished and perhaps reflect your personal style or brand preferences. You also want to explore customizing the colors to make it more visually appealing, maybe check the data your app collects too.

### 📖 Theory: Visual and Data Customization

Spark provides several ways to customize your application without requiring technical knowledge:

- **Theme customization**: Easy visual adjustments using the Theme pane to modify colors, fonts, spacing, and overall appearance
- **Asset management**: Upload and incorporate images, logos, or other files into your application
- **Data storage**: Spark automatically provides key-value storage for your app's data, with a built-in interface to view and manage stored information
- **AI capabilities**: Embed intelligent features like chat bots, content generation, and smart automation without complex integrations

> 💡 **Tip:** Before you start making an AI chatbot for answering support questions, check out Copilot Spaces first.

To learn more about customizing your app, check out the [styling documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/tutorials/spark/build-apps-with-spark#step-3-customize-the-styling-of-your-app) and [data management guide](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/tutorials/spark/build-apps-with-spark#step-4-store-and-manage-data).

### ⌨️ Activity: Personalize Your Design

1. Click the "Theme" tab and experiment with different color schemes and typography options

1. Try different pre-generated themes and observe how they change your app's appearance

1. Customize the colors and styling based on your app type

1. Make final adjustments to spacing, button styles, and other visual elements to perfect your app's look

### ⌨️ Activity: View Stored Data

1. Click on the "Data" tab to show a list of data tables.

   - Notice the sample data we asked it to include.

1. In the window, find an entry and click the edit icon.

1. Change the value and click the Update button.

1. Check the application to see if the content changed.

1. Comment below describing what theme changes you made and what data you found in your app. Maybe include a screenshot!

<details>
<summary>Having trouble? 🤷</summary><br/>

- The Theme tab is located at the top of the Spark interface - look for tabs next to "Preview"
- Pre-generated themes are available as quick starting points before making custom adjustments
- The Data tab shows all the information your app stores - you can edit entries directly from this interface
- Changes to themes appear immediately in the live preview

</details>
